Note: per user request, consult refs/graphviz sources instead of re-inventing algorithms.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Replaced recursive slice-based simplify_polyline with index/stack RDP (keep-array) to avoid quadratic allocations on long polylines. Mirrors Graphviz pathplan-style index recursion (refs/graphviz/lib/pathplan/route.c) per user suggestion to consult official sources.

title: Reduce remaining complex.dot left-chain x drift
description: After zeroing nodesep for vnodes, most nodes match Graphviz but the left chain (z_req/mobvm/machsrv) is still off by ~20-30pt. Investigate whether record port offsets, edge constraints, or cluster containment cause the remaining drift.
notes: Measured current drift: z_req/z_req1 are +18.52pt vs Graphviz (pos 220.52/100.52 vs 202/82). Likely due to missing cluster rankleader handling in x-constraints (Graphviz class2/cluster.c). Tried heuristic leader/offset approaches locally; reverted due to regressions.

title: X-order reorder too slow with large vnode counts
description: Large graphs with many edge vnodes spend seconds reordering x-groups after mincross. Example: refs/graphviz/tests/1718.dot takes ~10s in MoonBit (vnodes=15072) vs ~7.5s in dot; DOT_TRACE shows ~6s between virtual slack edges and xpos cluster constraints. Graphviz does mincross after class2 and uses the ordering directly for dot_position (see refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/mincross.c:init_mincross and refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/position.c), so we should avoid the extra x-group reorder/transpose when vnode counts are large.
notes: Reference Graphviz: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/mincross.c (class2/mincross ordering) and refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/position.c (position uses ND_order).

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Implemented guard to skip x-group reorder/transpose when vnode_count exceeds MAX_XPOS_REORDER_VNODES (5000). This avoids the extra O(N^2) x-order pass after mincross, aligning with Graphviz’s single mincross ordering. DOT_TRACE on refs/graphviz/tests/1718.dot dropped total layout from ~10.1s to ~3.9s (xpos reorder gap removed; network simplex ~3.3s).

notes: Record layout now computes record sizes, rects attr, and port-based edge anchoring; records.gv restored in xdot snapshot parity with rects presence checked (numeric geometry still skipped).

title: xdot: arrowhead shapes parity
description: Implement non-normal arrowhead shapes and re-enable arrows.gv xdot snapshot coverage (currently skipped in src/render/xdot/snapshot_test.mbt).

title: xdot: tighten geometry/text comparisons
description: Restore numeric geometry checks for bb/pos and add _ldraw_ text baseline parity in xdot snapshots once layout/text metrics converge; current snapshot_test skips numeric values and _ldraw_.

title: SVG renderer: record shapes + field labels
description: Add record shape rendering in svg output. Handle record label splitting (respect escaped pipes/ports), draw outline + separators, and place field labels. Add/extend svg tests using records.gv.

title: Dot layout: support splines=polyline/ortho/none
description: Extend dot layout to honor splines=polyline/ortho/none. Decide representation (degenerate beziers or new polyline type), update xdot/svg renderers accordingly, and add layout/render tests.
notes: Layout options default now leaves fields None so graph attributes (splines/rankdir/nodesep/ranksep) take effect. Added ortho vs polyline test using multi-edge graph.

title: Dot layout: honor rank constraints and edge weights
description: Implement rank constraints (subgraph rank=same/min/max/source/sink), edge constraint=false handling, and edge weight/minlen parsing for network_simplex. Add tests for rank constraints and weights.
notes: constraint attribute treats false/0/no as non-constraint; edge weights parsed to Int (>=0). Still missing rank=min/max/source/sink handling.

title: CLI: engine dispatch + neato -n/-x support
description: Dispatch layout based on engine (dot/neato/fdp/etc). Add no-op layout for neato -n/-x using pos/pin attributes and optional pruning. Add CLI tests for engine selection and -n behavior.

title: Document cgraph data model and attributes
description:
Goal: write docs/graphviz/cgraph-model.md summarizing graph/node/edge structures, attribute storage, and read/write flows.
Deliverable: data model notes + key type map for MoonBit port.
References: refs/graphviz/lib/cgraph, refs/graphviz/doc/libgraph/Agraph.tex, refs/graphviz/lib/cdt, refs/graphviz/lib/common.

title: Attributes module: typed parsing + defaults
description: Implement attrs package for defaults and typed parsing (colors, fonts, sizes, styles). Replace ad-hoc parsing in layout/render with shared helpers and add unit tests.

title: Document layout engines and constraints
description:
Goal: write docs/graphviz/layouts.md describing each layout engine and its inputs/outputs.
Deliverable: per-engine summary (dot, neato, fdp, sfdp, twopi, circo, osage, patchwork) and key shared components.
References: refs/graphviz/doc/libguide/layouts.tex, refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen, refs/graphviz/lib/neatogen, refs/graphviz/lib/fdpgen, refs/graphviz/lib/sfdpgen, refs/graphviz/lib/twopigen, refs/graphviz/lib/circogen, refs/graphviz/lib/osage, refs/graphviz/lib/patchwork, refs/graphviz/lib/vpsc.

title: Document edge routing, labels, and path planning
description:
Goal: write docs/graphviz/edge-routing.md for edge routing, spline generation, label placement, and overlap removal.
Deliverable: flow diagram + notes on shared geometry utilities.
References: refs/graphviz/lib/pathplan/README, refs/graphviz/lib/ortho, refs/graphviz/lib/edgepaint, refs/graphviz/lib/label, refs/graphviz/lib/xdot, refs/graphviz/doc/libguide/paths.tex, refs/graphviz/doc/libguide/overlap.tex.

title: Document rendering pipeline and plugin system
description:
Goal: write docs/graphviz/rendering.md covering gvc lifecycle, renderers/devices, and plugin discovery.
Deliverable: render pipeline summary with plugin/format map.
References: refs/graphviz/doc/libguide/drivers.tex, refs/graphviz/doc/libguide/plugins.tex, refs/graphviz/plugin, refs/graphviz/lib/gvc, refs/graphviz/lib/common, refs/graphviz/lib/xdot.

title: Inventory CLI tools and I/O formats
description:
Goal: write docs/graphviz/cli-and-formats.md listing core binaries, flags, and supported input/output formats.
Deliverable: table mapping commands to layout engines and formats.
References: refs/graphviz/cmd, refs/graphviz/doc/Dot.ref, refs/graphviz/graphviz.7, refs/graphviz/doc/index.html.

title: Select test corpus and parity checks

Deliverable: curated list with expected outputs and priority tiers.

title: Define MoonBit rewrite scope and module plan

Goal: write docs/graphviz/port-plan.md defining MVP scope, package layout, and staging plan for the MoonBit rewrite.
Deliverable: phased roadmap + initial module/package map.
References: docs created by graphviz.mbt-b0p.1 through graphviz.mbt-b0p.8.

title: dot layout too slow on large graphs (1864.dot >120s)
description: Running dot layout on refs/graphviz/tests/1864.dot takes >120s in graphviz.mbt (timeout). This is far slower than official Graphviz dot; need to find perf bottleneck and align algorithm/data structures.
notes: Large 1864.dot now completes (~9s) by skipping vnodes in x-position network simplex when no edge labels/ports and vnode count > MAX_XPOS_VNODES, then interpolating vnode x-positions; this avoids ~600k-node constraint graphs. Also replaced recursive inter_tree_edge_search (see refs/graphviz/lib/common/ns.c) with explicit stack to prevent stack overflow on large graphs. HTML labels now lexed from '<' like refs/graphviz/lib/cgraph/scan.l.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Checked refs/graphviz behavior: HTML labels should take precedence even on record shapes; updated layout to treat HTML labels first, skip record rects/ports for HTML labels, cache parsed labels/ports, and skip port offset/label-port work when no explicit ports. Large refs/graphviz/tests/1864.dot still >120s; likely remaining hotspot in layout/network_simplex or html table sizing (see refs/graphviz/lib/common/htmltable.c, lib/common/ns.c).

title: Layout: restore bb parity for grammar.gv
description: Layout snapshot for grammar.gv still differs (bb x-extent off vs Graphviz). Investigate rank ordering/spacing or bbox expansion and restore parity so dot layout snapshots pass.

title: Extend layout validation tests for options and neato node sizes
description: Add regression coverage for layout_dot rejecting negative nodesep/ranksep passed via DotLayoutOptions and layout_neato rejecting negative node width/height.

title: Bug: MechCmd edge labels overlap in complex.svg
description: In tests/layout/dot/complex.dot, the two "MechCmd" edge labels overlap in the rendered SVG. Should align with official Graphviz label positions without overlap.
close_reason: Fixed complex layout constraints; MechCmd labels no longer overlap.

title: Add svg snapshot coverage for complex.dot
description: Add svg snapshot test for complex.dot (and maybe smaller labeled-edge graphs) to ensure renderer output stays aligned with Graphviz reference svg once x-spacing is fixed.

title: Add HTML label parser tests
description: Cover HTML label unescaping and table parsing in layout/html package.

title: Resolve unused home_graph_id field warning
description: moon check reports unused_field for home_graph_id in src/cgraph/model.mbt. Decide whether to remove the field, use it, or suppress the warning; update model/tests accordingly.

title: Graphviz parity plan: unit-fixture extraction first
description: Strategy: keep integration snapshots #skip until unit parity is exact (no float tolerance). Align layout/dot base functions against official Graphviz by reusing upstream unit tests when available; otherwise instrument refs/graphviz to capture inputs/outputs from running integration cases and convert into unit fixtures. For text measurement, allow hardcoded metrics for integration cases to match Graphviz exactly. When outputs diverge, consult refs/graphviz source instead of re-inventing algorithms (note per guidance).

title: xpos: mirror class2 vnode chain merging
description: Extract/align Graphviz class2 vnode chain creation and merge logic so constraint graph vnodes match xpos.jsonl. Implement mergeable/merge_chain behavior for parallel and intercluster edges (same tail/head, label, ports) and ensure vnode lw/rw match Graphviz plain_vnode/label_vnode.
notes: Implemented Graphviz-style merge_chain: edge_vnode_key excludes labeled edges (label -> unique key), vnode widths incremented by nodesep/2 when merging chains; all edge_vnodes lookups now use edge_vnode_key. Refs: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/class2.c (merge_chain/mergeable/plain_vnode), refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/fastgr.c (virtual_node).

title: xpos: model cluster/flat virtual nodes (lw=1)
description: Identify and implement Graphviz virtual nodes with lw/rw=1 that appear in xpos_input (cluster rankleaders or flat slots). Add generation to constraint graph builder and ensure ordering/ranks match fixture.
notes: Refs: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/cluster.c build_skeleton/remove_rankleaders; refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/flat.c make_vn_slot/flat_node. Fixture: tests/fixtures/graphviz/xpos.jsonl graph=yours_truly has 8 virtual nodes with lw/rw=1 at ranks 0/7/14/41/48/191/281.

title: xpos: align GD_rank ordering with Graphviz ranks list
description: Ensure x_groups ordering (with vnodes) matches Graphviz GD_rank order from xpos.jsonl. Compare our x_groups to fixture ranks list and adjust vnode insertion keys / ordering logic accordingly.
notes: Refs: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/mincross.c (build_ranks, transpose), position.c make_LR_constraints uses GD_rank order. Fixture: tests/fixtures/graphviz/xpos.jsonl includes ranks arrays for each graph.

title: xpos: include vnodes in slack-edge generation
description: Align make_edge_pairs parity by generating slack nodes for all edges (including virtual edges along vnode chains). Remove skip that skipped edges with vnodes in constraint graph build.
notes: Ref: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/position.c make_edge_pairs loops ND_save_out (includes virtual edges).
close_reason: Implemented: constraint graph now keeps edges with vnodes so slack nodes are generated for virtual edges.

title: xpos: use Graphviz plain_vnode widths & include non-constraint edges
description: Align vnode generation with Graphviz class2: build vnodes for all edges (not only constraint=true) and use plain_vnode widths (lw/rw = 1 + nodesep/2) for non-label vnodes.
notes: Ref: refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/class2.c plain_vnode/label_vnode.
close_reason: Implemented: build_edge_vnodes/build_vnode_order_keys use all edges and plain_vnode widths.

title: Dot layout still slow on large graphs (optimize x-order + vnode constraints like Graphviz)
description: Large DOT layouts remain slow (e.g. refs/graphviz/tests/1864.dot ~30s on our CLI). We should align x-ordering and constraint handling with Graphviz to avoid extra work and incorrect constraints.
notes: Deprioritized to P3 per request; moving focus to other issues.
close_reason: Superseded by P0 base-function parity plan (graphviz.mbt-ko0).

title: SVG spot-check outputs / dot layout too slow (moon test appears hung)
notes: Root cause found: acyclic_dfs reverses self-loop edges (tail==head) and re-adds them to the same adjacency list, causing an infinite loop on graphs like fsm.gv/train11.gv. Fixed by skipping reversal for self-loops; aligns with Graphviz's acyclic behavior (refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/acyclic.c).
close_reason: Fixed infinite loop in acyclic DFS on self-loop edges; svg spot-check outputs no longer hangs.

title: complex.svg: MechCmd edge labels overlap
description: In tests/render/svg/complex.svg, the two MechCmd edge labels overlap each other; align placement to match Graphviz (no overlap).

title: Add compound cluster edge regression fixture
description: Select a small compound=true DOT from refs/graphviz/tests (e.g., 1453.dot or 1332.dot) and add layout + svg/xdot baselines to cover lhead/ltail cluster edge routing.
close_reason: Added compound cluster fixture with layout/svg/xdot baselines

title: Investigate svg/xdot parity gaps on fig6
description: Compare our render_svg/render_xdot output vs Graphviz dot CLI for fig6.gv and note any mismatches; file bug issues if needed.

title: Wrong edge curve degree vs official Graphviz
description: Edges render with overly curved splines compared to official dot output; visually too large curvature (e.g., complex/records snapshots).
notes: Disabled port_side_anchor for record-shaped explicit ports so clipping stays on record field centers. Graphviz record ports use dynamic compass on field boxes (refs/graphviz/lib/common/shapes.c record_port/compassPort), so skipping side-anchor heuristics avoids pulling edges onto the wrong record row. Records.gv edge diffs dropped (a:bala->b:left now ~19pt vs 63pt).

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Reviewed Graphviz sources in refs/graphviz (lib/pathplan/route.c, lib/common/routespl.c, lib/common/splines.c) to compare spline fitting with Proutespline/splinefits. Remaining mismatch likely needs closer adherence to Graphviz constraint/fit logic.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Adjusted line-edge spline constraints to mirror Graphviz routesplines: only apply endpoint slopes when ports are explicitly constrained (routesplines_ sets evs only if start/end constrained). Unconstrained edges now pass zero slopes to route_polyline_to_bezier to reduce over-curvature.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: For line edges with multi-edge curvature offsets, switched from bulged control points to perpendicular offset straight splines, matching Graphviz makeStraightEdges (routespl.c) which offsets entire edge by nodesep along the perpendicular.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Aligned spline fitting closer to Graphviz route.c: added control-point distance-to-polyline checks and nodesep-based bound_eps when fitting splines (see refs/graphviz/lib/pathplan/route.c + lib/common/routespl.c). This reduces over-curved edges when our polyline detours too far, but large deviations remain where our polyline routing differs from Graphviz (e.g., complex dotted edges). Next step likely needs closer routing parity with Graphviz pathplan boxes.

comments.author: Wen Yuxiang
comments.text: Implemented spline fit tightening to approximate Graphviz's splineisinside (refs/graphviz/lib/pathplan/route.c) by sampling curve points vs polyline corridor, and reduced bound_eps + densified long segments before route_polyline_to_bezier. This should reduce over-curvature when our polyline is coarse, but complex.xdot still shows large deviations dominated by x-position differences (see graphviz.mbt-2lj).

title: Investigate remaining complex.dot intercluster edge deltas

close_reason: Adjusted edge label placement for straight TB/BT edges; regenerated svg/xdot baselines to avoid overlap.

title: Avoid duplicate spline sampling for edge label placement
description: edge_label_pos samples spline points twice per labeled edge (midpoint + axis), which adds avoidable overhead on labeled graphs. Reuse the 96-step samples and derive the 48-step axis samples by downsampling to keep output identical but remove redundant work.
notes: Graphviz places edge labels from label vnodes (refs/graphviz/lib/dotgen/dotsplines.c place_vnlabel) without resampling; this change at least removes our extra sampling while keeping current outputs.
close_reason: Reuse 96-step spline samples and downsample for axis placement to avoid duplicate sampling without changing label positions.
